AgentDetail.description:
Detail page payload — same metrics + recent traces + violation
breakdown by policy.
AgentDetail.title: AgentDetail

AgentSummary.description: One card on the agent grid.
AgentSummary.title: AgentSummary

LabelRequest.description:
Body for POST /v1/labels — operator marks a trace/span as bad/good
for downstream classifier training and false-positive review.

field.description: One of: input, output, tool_params, tool_result
field.title: Field

LabelRequest.title: LabelRequest

PolicyAuditEntry.description: One row from the policy_audit log.
PolicyAuditEntry.title: PolicyAuditEntry

PolicyCreate.description:
Body for POST /v1/policies (Phase 4).

Agent Firewall fields (lifecycle/mode/priority/...) are optional —
when omitted they take spec defaults. Notably ``mode`` defaults to
``shadow`` per §10.1 of AGENT_FIREWALL_SPEC.md: a freshly authored
rule never blocks live traffic until an operator promotes it.
PolicyCreate.title: PolicyCreate

PolicyOut.description:
Policy as returned by GET /v1/policies + GET /v1/policies/{name}.

Mirrors the YAML/DB shape but is the only schema that crosses the
API boundary — the SDK's internal ``Policy`` dataclass stays in
the SDK.
PolicyOut.title: PolicyOut

PolicyUpdate.title: PolicyUpdate

PolicyViolation.description: One violation as returned by GET /v1/violations.
PolicyViolation.title: PolicyViolation

PolicyViolationInput.description: One violation as accepted by POST /v1/violations from the SDK.
PolicyViolationInput.title: PolicyViolationInput

PromoteSuggestionRequest.description:
Body for POST /v1/policies/suggest/{id}/promote. The operator
can rename the policy at promotion time — the auto-generated name
is fine but operators usually want something descriptive.
PromoteSuggestionRequest.title: PromoteSuggestionRequest

RedactContextDetectors.description:
Per-detector toggles for the L3 redactor.

Plugin-side operators can disable specific detectors without
uninstalling Presidio or removing vault data. ``None`` means
"use the server default (all on)".

See TENANT_ISOLATION_SPEC §2.4 detector matrix.
RedactContextDetectors.title: RedactContextDetectors

confirm.description: MUST be true. Required acknowledgement that restore overwrites the live database.
confirm.title: Confirm

SpanInput.description:
Span as accepted by POST /v1/spans. Many fields are optional so that
minimal payloads (the curl example in the session prompt) and rich
payloads (LangChain integration) both work.
SpanInput.title: SpanInput

SuggestRequest.description:
Body for POST /v1/policies/suggest. Operator clicks "Block this
pattern" on a fired decision; we synthesize a draft policy.
SuggestRequest.title: SuggestRequest

TemplateInstantiateRequest.description:
Body for POST /v1/firewall/templates/{id}/instantiate.

The dashboard renders the template's ``fields`` schema as a form,
collects the operator's choices, and POSTs them here. The server
compiles the condition + creates the policy in mode=shadow per
§10.1 (operator promotes via ModeToggle after reviewing forecast).
TemplateInstantiateRequest.title: TemplateInstantiateRequest

TestSuiteRequest.description:
Body for POST /v1/firewall/test/cases. The shape mirrors the
on-disk YAML format documented in firewall.test_runner.
TestSuiteRequest.title: TestSuiteRequest

TraceDetail.description:
Trace shape returned by GET /v1/traces/{id} — same as Trace
plus a compact list of policy violations attached to the trace
(empty when the Policy Engine isn't in use). The list endpoint
/v1/traces stays on the simpler Trace shape so its payload size
is unchanged.
TraceDetail.title: TraceDetail

TraceViolationSummary.description: Compact form embedded in GET /v1/traces/{id} responses.
TraceViolationSummary.title: TraceViolationSummary

info.description: Local-first AI agent observability — ingest and query API
info.title: Korveo API

post.description:
Snapshot the DuckDB to a named directory under KORVEO_BACKUP_DIR.

Default name is the UTC timestamp (``snap_YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ``)
so unattended cron jobs don't have to think up names.

schema.title: Response Delete Backup V1 Admin Backups  Name  Delete

post.description:
Restore the named snapshot. **Destructive** — overwrites
every table in the live DB.

Caller must pass ``{"confirm": true}``. Anything else 400s so a
misfired curl can't accidentally roll back the database.

schema.title: Response Restore Backup V1 Admin Backups  Name  Restore Post

get.description:
Return the active firewall profile + per-toggle overrides for
the given agent. Defaults to the ``_default`` row when no
agent-specific config exists. Returned shape is what the plugin
expects to merge.

**Auth**: gated by the global ``BearerAuthMiddleware``. When
``KORVEO_API_TOKEN`` is set, callers must include
``Authorization: Bearer <token>``. When unset, this endpoint is
open — fine for ``localhost``-bound dev, NOT for any deployment
that exposes the API beyond loopback.

put.description:
Upsert the firewall profile + overrides. Validates the profile
name and filters overrides to known toggle keys so a typo or
malicious payload can't smuggle hidden fields. Plugin picks up
the change on its next poll (~30s by default).

get.description:
Per-component health for the dashboard's ops page.

Dashboard's existing ``/health`` is a 200/200 binary — useful
for the Docker healthcheck, useless when something is degraded
but technically responding. This breaks it down per subsystem.

Single-shot snapshot of every Korveo self-metric. Designed
for periodic scraping (every 30s is plenty).

schema.title: Response Admin Metrics V1 Admin Metrics Get

post.description:
Run the cleanup task once with an explicit cutoff.

Useful for operators who want to trim a runaway DB without
waiting for the next scheduled tick (default daily). The env
var ``KORVEO_RETENTION_DAYS`` is unaffected — this is one-shot.

get.description:
List all distinct agents (= distinct trace.name) and their
activity metrics over the configured window.

The query is one DuckDB aggregate — no per-agent N+1. Joins against
spans for cost / token / model / error_rate, against
policy_violations for the violation count.

parameters.description: Filter by framework: openclaw / mastra / voltagent / default
parameters.name: project

schema.title: Project

schema.title: Provider

get.description:
One agent's detail page: same metrics as the card, plus the
most-recent traces and a per-policy violation breakdown so the
operator can scan what's been firing.

schema.title: Response List Approvals V1 Approvals Get

get.description:
Recent bypasses — labels marked ``bad`` on traces where no
block-class decision fired. Default window: last 30 days.

schema.title: Response List Bypasses Endpoint V1 Firewall Bypasses Get

get.description:
Aggregate bypass counts by category, tool, and agent. Used
by the dashboard banner / coverage page.

schema.title: Response Bypass Summary Endpoint V1 Firewall Bypasses Summary Get

List all trained classifier versions. Used by the dashboard
classifier page.

Train a fresh classifier over the labels table. Returns the
new version's training summary. 503 when sklearn isn't
installed; 400 on too-few labels or unsupported backend.

schema.title: Response Retrain Classifier Endpoint V1 Firewall Classifier Retrain Post

get.description: All corpora with their entry counts. Cheap aggregate query.

schema.title: Response List Corpora Endpoint V1 Firewall Corpora Get

Entries for a corpus. Returns text + created_at — embeddings
are not exposed (1.5KB BLOBs aren't useful to the dashboard).

schema.title: Response List Corpus Entries Endpoint V1 Firewall Corpora  Name  Entries Get

instead of silent failure.

schema.title: Response Add Corpus Entry Endpoint V1 Firewall Corpora  Name  Entries Post

get.description: Recent drift alerts. Used by the dashboard banner.

schema.title: Response List Drift Alerts V1 Firewall Drift Alerts Get
Response Ack Drift Alert V1 Firewall Drift Alerts  Alert Id  Acknowledge Post

schema.title: Response Run Drift V1 Firewall Drift Run Post

get.description:
List every starter pack available for import. Returns one
object per pack: pack_id, display name, category, policy count,
short description, lifecycle list, auto-installed flag.

The dashboard's /firewall/library page renders this verbatim;
operators get a one-click install per pack.

schema.title: Response List Library Packs Endpoint V1 Firewall Library Get

get.description:
Preview a pack's policies without writing anything. Used by
the import-confirmation modal so operators see what they're
about to install.

schema.title: Response Preview Library Pack Endpoint V1 Firewall Library  Pack Id  Get

post.description:
Import every policy in a pack into the DB. Idempotent —
duplicate policy names are SKIPPED (operator's existing rule
wins). All imported policies land in mode=shadow per §10.1.

schema.title: Response Import Library Pack Endpoint V1 Firewall Library  Pack Id  Import Post

post.description:
Manual trigger — kicks the frequent-pattern miner on demand.
Same scan + emit logic as the background loop. Returns a summary
of what it did. Useful for ad-hoc operator-driven scans
("refresh suggestions now").

schema.title: Response Run Miner V1 Firewall Miner Run Post
Response Get Panic Disabled V1 Firewall Panic Disable Get

post.description:
Operator big-red-button: turn off all enforcement immediately.

Persisted to ``firewall_kv`` so a restart picks the disabled state
back up. Re-enabling is the same endpoint with disabled=false.

Auditable — writes a row in ``firewall_panic_audit`` (created
inline if absent so the panic surface doesn't depend on a
migration the operator might not have run yet).

schema.title: Response Set Panic Disabled V1 Firewall Panic Disable Post

post.description:
Scrub every text in ``texts`` of structured secrets that
do NOT belong to ``user_id``, returning the redacted
versions. Catches:

  1. Vault entries known to belong to other users (exact
     excerpt match — same surface as
     ``/v1/firewall/vault/foreign-excerpts``).
  2. Structured-ID patterns (account numbers, customer IDs)
     present in the texts that AREN'T in the current user's
     vault. Closes the v0.6.1 brutal-test gap where
     ``DEMO-12345`` leaked because it was sent before
     user_id propagation worked, so it never landed in the
     vault under any user — yet the LLM still had it in
     conversation history. Pattern-based defense doesn't
     need prior recording.

The current user's OWN vault excerpts pass through
unchanged so they can keep referring to their own
account number.

Used by ``@korveo/openclaw-diagnostics`` from
``before_prompt_build`` to scrub the LLM's prompt + history
before the model runs, so a foreign user's secret can't
enter the model's context. This is the only architecturally
sound point to enforce cross-session isolation — output-
side rewriting was provably leaky on Slack
(``chat.postMessage`` flashes the original before
``chat.update`` redacts it).

schema.title: Response Redact Context Endpoint V1 Firewall Redact Context Post

get.description:
Pending pattern suggestions for the dashboard inbox.
state ∈ pending | promoted | dismissed | all.

schema.title: Response List Suggestions V1 Firewall Suggestions Get

get.description:
All available rule templates. Cheap — registry is loaded once at
module load. Returns a compact summary; the dashboard fetches the
full template (with fields) on click via the {id} endpoint.

schema.title: Response List Templates V1 Firewall Templates Get

get.description:
Full template — fields schema, defaults, condition string. The
dashboard's modal form renders ``fields`` as a form.

schema.title: Response Get Template Detail V1 Firewall Templates  Template Id  Get

post.description:
Create a policy from a template + operator's field values.

Always lands in mode=shadow per §10.1 unless operator explicitly
asks for another mode. Triggers an engine reload so the new rule
is live in the same response — same pattern as POST /v1/policies.

schema.title: Response Instantiate Template V1 Firewall Templates  Template Id  Instantiate Post

get.description:
Return the list of attack categories the generator supports.
Used by the dashboard's "Generate test attacks" UI.

schema.title: Response List Attack Categories V1 Firewall Test Attack Categories Get

post.description:
Run a rule unit-test suite against the current engine.

Returns ``{policy, total, passed, failed, results: [...]}``. Each
result has ``name``, ``passed``, and (on failure) ``expected``,
``actual``, ``actual_policy``, ``actual_reason``. Caller compares
``failed > 0`` against zero for a pass/fail signal.

400 on malformed suite (missing policy / tests / expect).

schema.title: Response Run Test Cases Endpoint V1 Firewall Test Cases Post

post.description:
Generate synthetic attack inputs operators can run through
the firewall (or their agent) to verify rule coverage.

Returns ``{count, attacks: [...]}``. Each attack is a dict in
decide()-request shape, with an extra ``expected_to`` field
listing decisions a properly-configured firewall should
produce. When the actual decision isn't in ``expected_to``,
that's a coverage gap the operator should investigate.

schema.title: Response Generate Attacks Endpoint V1 Firewall Test Generate Attacks Post

post.description:
Replay ``trace_id`` against the current engine. Returns a
structured per-span decision list + summary counts.

404 when the trace doesn't exist or has no spans. 400 on
invalid request shape. The replay endpoint is read-only — no
decisions / approvals / circuit-breaker state are written.

schema.title: Response Replay Endpoint V1 Firewall Test Replay Post

get.description:
List session-vault entries (the cross-session leak detector's
DB). Filter by user_id / session_id; default 100 most recent.

The full fact value was never stored — operators see only the
truncated excerpt + metadata so a vault dump can't re-leak the
sensitive data it was protecting.

schema.title: Response List Vault Entries Endpoint V1 Firewall Vault Get

get.description:
Return the de-duplicated list of vault fact excerpts that
DO NOT belong to ``user_id`` — i.e. the secrets a leak rule
would catch if they appeared in the LLM's outbound text for
this user.

Brutal-test fix v0.6.1 (2026-05-10): the OpenClaw plugin
needs this list to client-side-redact prompts BEFORE the LLM
runs, so a foreign user's secret never enters the model's
context in the first place. Output-side rewriting was
fundamentally leaky (Slack ``chat.update`` flashes the
original to the recipient before the redaction lands), so
cross-session isolation has to happen at the input layer
where the firewall hook IS awaited and the LLM sees the
redacted prompt directly.

The endpoint deliberately does NOT return the foreign user_id
or session_id — only the excerpts. A plugin scrubbing
against this list shouldn't need to know whose secret it is,
only that it must not appear.

schema.title: Response Vault Foreign Excerpts Endpoint V1 Firewall Vault Foreign Excerpts Get

get.description:
Aggregate counts for the dashboard's vault overview card —
total entries, breakdown by fact_kind, top-20 user_ids by
entry count.

schema.title: Response Vault Stats Endpoint V1 Firewall Vault Stats Get

delete.description:
Erase a single vault entry. Useful for right-to-erasure
(GDPR Art. 17) and for cleaning up false-positive recordings
that would noise up the leak detector.

schema.title: Response Delete Vault Entry Endpoint V1 Firewall Vault  Entry Id  Delete

get.description:
List configured webhook destinations. Secret config fields
(tokens, HMAC keys) are masked in the response — operators see
enough to identify the row but not exfiltrate the secret.

schema.title: Response List Firewall Webhooks Endpoint V1 Firewall Webhooks Get

post.description:
Create a new webhook destination. Returns the row id; the
secret config field comes back masked on subsequent GETs.

schema.title: Response Create Firewall Webhook Endpoint V1 Firewall Webhooks Post

get.description:
Webhook DLQ — deliveries that exhausted retries. Operators
inspect this when a Slack / PagerDuty channel goes dark.

schema.title: Response List Webhook Failures Endpoint V1 Firewall Webhooks Failures Get
Response Delete Firewall Webhook Endpoint V1 Firewall Webhooks  Webhook Id  Delete

post.description:
Insert a label row. Used by the dashboard's 'Mark as false
positive' button on /decisions/{id}, and as the substrate for
the local classifier trainer (Slice 4).

schema.title: Response Post Label V1 Labels Post

post.description:
Forward an OpenAI-compatible request to the configured upstream
and capture a Korveo span on the way back.

Path is preserved verbatim under the upstream base, so a client
pointed at ``http://localhost:8000/v1/openai`` sees the upstream
surface unchanged: ``/v1/chat/completions``, ``/v1/embeddings``,
``/v1/models``, etc. all work.

Streaming responses (``stream: true``) are passed through chunk
by chunk; we buffer in parallel to assemble the span at the end.
Non-streaming responses are returned in a single Response.

Errors from the upstream are returned as-is; a span is still
emitted so operators can see failed calls in the dashboard.

OTLP/HTTP traces endpoint.

Content-Type resolution:
  - ``application/x-protobuf`` → protobuf decode (the wire default
    for OTel SDK exporters and the OTel collector).
  - ``application/json`` → JSON decode (manual curl, dashboards,
    easier to inspect during debugging).
  - Anything else → try protobuf first, fall back to a 400 if
    that also fails. OTel SDKs default to protobuf and sometimes
    send no Content-Type at all.

Project resolution:
  - ``X-Korveo-Project`` header (operator-set; wins)
  - Resource attribute ``service.name`` (auto-populated by every
    OTel SDK from ``OTEL_SERVICE_NAME`` or the program name)
  - Default: NULL (read paths coalesce to "default")

Both go through ``otlp_decode.normalize_project`` so the closed
framework allowlist applies — keeps the /agents grid headlines
stable when third-party tools emit arbitrary service names.

get.description:
List currently active policies.

DB-backed when the engine is sourced from the policies table; falls
through to the in-memory engine state when YAML is the source.

The ``?agent=`` filter narrows to rules that would actually fire
for events from that agent — the dashboard's AgentDetail page
calls this to show the "Active policies" section.

parameters.description: Filter to policies that apply to this agent name
parameters.name: agent

schema.description: Filter to policies that apply to this agent name
schema.title: Agent

post.description:
Create a new policy. Validates against the engine's grammar
(trigger / action / severity enums) AND parses the condition
through SimpleEval to reject unparseable expressions.

On success: writes the row, bumps the engine's version watcher,
triggers an immediate reload so the new rule is live within the
response. The dashboard sees the new policy without a poll.

Generate a draft policy from a fired decision. Persists the
suggestion in the pattern_suggestions table so operators can
revisit / dismiss / promote later.

schema.title: Response Suggest Policy V1 Policies Suggest Post
Response Get Suggestion V1 Policies Suggest  Suggestion Id  Get
Response Dismiss Suggestion V1 Policies Suggest  Suggestion Id  Dismiss Post

post.description: Promote a suggestion into a real policy. Lands in mode=shadow.

schema.title: Response Promote Suggestion V1 Policies Suggest  Suggestion Id  Promote Post

get.description:
Single policy by name. DB row when DB-backed, else from the
in-memory YAML engine state.

put.description:
Partial update of a policy. Field-level — omitted fields keep
their current value.

get.description: Audit log for one policy. Most recent first.

schema.title: Response Set Policy Mode V1 Policies  Name  Mode Post

post.description:
Restore a policy to an earlier version snapshot.

Body: ``{"version_number": N}``. Operator-attributed via the
standard X-Korveo-Actor header so the rollback itself appears in
the audit log.

schema.title: Response Rollback Policy V1 Policies  Name  Rollback Post

Version snapshots — every create / update / rollback writes
a row to ``policy_versions``. The dashboard's history tab
renders this as a timeline with one-click rollback.

schema.title: Response List Policy Versions V1 Policies  Name  Versions Get

get.description: Fetch a specific historical version (full YAML snapshot).

schema.title: Response Get Policy Version V1 Policies  Name  Versions  Version Number  Get

post.description: Synchronous decision endpoint. Never raises — Rule 7.

schema.title: Response Decide Endpoint V1 Policy Decide Post

get.description:
Snapshot of engine counters + p50/p99 eval latency.

Designed for ad-hoc curl + dashboard polling. Doesn't persist
anything — all numbers are in-process state. Restart resets.

schema.title: Response Get Metrics V1 Policy Metrics Get

post.description:
Force a hot-reload of KORVEO_POLICY_FILE.

On invalid YAML the previous engine stays in place — production-
safety choice over fail-open. Caller sees ``{"ok": false, ...}``
and can re-fix the file before the next call.

schema.title: Response Post Reload V1 Policy Reload Post
Response List Sessions V1 Sessions Get

post.description:
Ingest a batch of spans.

The optional ``X-Korveo-Project`` header lets the integration declare
which framework it is (set by every TS exporter — openclaw / mastra
/ voltagent — and the Python SDK config). We persist it on each
span + propagate to the trace so the agent grid can group by
framework. Empty / missing header → project stays NULL, treated
as "default" downstream.

parameters.description: Multi-tenant scope. When set, returns only traces tagged with this project. Use 'default' for the un-tagged bucket. Omit to see all projects (operator view).
parameters.name: project

schema.description: Multi-tenant scope. When set, returns only traces tagged with this project. Use 'default' for the un-tagged bucket. Omit to see all projects (operator view).
schema.title: Project
Response List Traces V1 Traces Get
Response Get Trace Spans V1 Traces  Trace Id  Spans Get

get.description: List violations with optional filters.

post.description:
Bulk insert violations from the SDK. Returns count accepted.

Webhook firing happens in the SDK, not here — by the time a
violation reaches this endpoint, ``webhook_fired`` already
reflects whether the SDK successfully called the webhook URL.

get.description: Aggregate counts. Cheap because the table is bounded by retention.
